name: leios_bait_and_switch_block
description: The server advertises EB1 via LeiosNotify block_offer (slot=100, hash=ab...) and then delivers a different block body via LeiosFetch when the client requests it. The delivered bytes are a CBOR empty map (0xa0) whose blake2b-256 hash is not ab..., so the block cannot possibly be the one that was offered. A conforming node must hash the received block bytes and verify the result matches the point it requested before storing or forwarding the block. Accepting the substituted content would allow a malicious peer to deliver arbitrary bytes under a trusted advertised identity and have them treated as a valid EB. The verifier should observe the node logging a hash mismatch and rejecting the block.
